text: ⚡️ 🇮🇷 🇷🇺 🇸🇦 🇶🇦 🇴🇲 🇪🇬 Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi held a phone call with Russian Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov to discuss the latest regional developments and the Iran–US negotiations in Islamabad, Pakistan. He also held separate calls with Saudi Foreign Minister Faisal bin Farhan, Qatari Prime Minister and Foreign Minister Mohammed bin Abdulrahman Al Thani, Omani Foreign Minister Badr Al-Busaidi, and Egyptian Foreign Minister Badr Abdel Aati. @wfwitness

⚡ 🇺🇸 The American carrier strike group centered around USS Gerald R. Ford was operating today east of Cyprus. She was already in the same area two days ago, she is not in transit and is expected to remain in the area until new orders are given. The carrier can be seen being refuelled by a supply ship. They are flanked by a pair of Arleigh Burke class destroyers. The European Space Agency providing the satellite imagery is delaying by a day the release of satellite imagery of the eastern Mediterranean sea where the European carrier group is operating. The American carrier strike group was caught here right on the edge. This is why the right part of the satellite view isn't loaded. CVN-78 33.75N 29.18E @wfwitness

⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 WSJ: The US blockade of Iranian trade through the Strait of Hormuz is pushing global oil markets into renewed turmoil, with supply already under severe strain. Around “2 million barrels per day” of Iranian oil exports could be removed from the market, intensifying a global scramble for crude. Oil prices have surged, with Brent crude rising to about $101 a barrel. Shipbrokers say that it isn't clear how ships can satisfy both US and Iranian conditions on leaving the Persian Gulf, and tanker owners say they will hesitate before sending new vessels through the Strait @wfwitness

🇺🇸 💬 🇮🇷 CNN: Trump officials are internally discussing details for a potential second, in-person meeting with Iranian officials before the ceasefire between Washington and Tehran expires next week, should the opportunity present itself, a source familiar with the talks told CNN, though it’s unclear whether such a meeting could materialize. 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

Visioner 🇺🇸 🇮🇩 🇨🇳 BIG | The United States is strengthening its positions in the Indo-Pacific to counter China’s dominance and is signing a strategic defense agreement with Indonesia. Secretary of War Pete Hegseth and Indonesia’s Minister of Defense Sjafrie Sjamsoeddin announced a new Major Defense Cooperation Partnership at the Pentagon, aimed at strengthening military ties and maintaining stability in the Indo-Pacific. ➡️ The agreement focuses on three pillars: capacity building, training and education, and joint operations. It also includes plans to co-develop advanced technologies, especially in maritime, subsurface, and autonomous systems, and expand special forces cooperation. ➡️ Both sides emphasized the growing relationship, which already includes over 170 joint exercises annually, and described the partnership as the start of a new phase in long-term U.S.-Indonesia defense cooperation. See the latest updates with us: @visionergeo tweet

Open Source Intel Péter Magyar: If Vladimir Putin calls me, I will pick up the phone. I do not think that will happen. I will not call him myself, but if we do talk, I can tell him to please stop the killing now, after four years, and end that war, which doesn't make any sense from their point of view either, because tens of thousands of Russians have lost their lives. I don't think he is going to end the war on my advice. I very much hope that he will be forced to do so regardless. tweet

OSINTdefender According to USNI News, citing two defense officials, the USS George H.W. Bush (CVN-77) and its escorts, USS Donald Cook (DDG-75), USS Mason (DDG-87) and USS Ross (DDG-71), did not transit the Strait of Gibraltar and are instead sailing around the southern tip of Africa. The carrier strike group is currently operating off the coast of Namibia, as it makes the trip avoiding the Suez Canal transit and the potentially contested zones of the Red Sea and Bab el-Mandeb Strait. tweet

🇱🇧 Hezbollah Secretary-General Sheikh Naim Qassem on attempts to create sectarian strife: "They are starting to promote that Turkey is creating a problem between Sunnis and Shia. Who is working on this? The Americans and the Israelis and Israel's servants internally. They want the strife to be between others, so they can get rid of both. We with our brothers the Sunnis are one heart. This strife, they will not succeed in it. Israel's servants will fail. We will not allow strife to raise its head. The Christians of all their sects and the Muslims of all their denominations — Sunnis and Druze and Alawites and all — are our brothers in the nation, in religion, and in the heavenly messages. What unites us is much stronger than the devils of the interior and exterior and the trumpets of strife." 🔴 @DDGeopolitics

BIG: A U.S. Army unit ran out of its ENTIRE stock of Precision Strike Missiles (PRSM) early in last month’s war with Iran. The missile — still in testing at the time — was used in combat for the first time and expended quickly. SOURCE

⚡️ 🇮🇱 🇵🇸 Security inspectors at the Shavei Shomron crossing stopped a garbage truck arriving from the east, where approximately 40 Palestinian illegal border crossers were found hiding in the waste compartment. Several were unconscious and are being treated by Magen David Adom. All suspects have been transferred to security forces. @wfwitness
Trump officials are considering a possible second in-person meeting with Iranian officials before the ceasefire expires next week, though plans are still early and uncertain. Source: CNN

The U.S. asked Iran to stop enriching uranium for 20 years during recent talks, but Iran pushed back, offering only a much shorter limit. The disagreement over uranium enrichment and Iran’s existing stockpile was the main reason no deal was reached. Source: Axios

⚡️ 🇺🇸 🇮🇷 CNN : Trump administration officials are internally discussing details for a potential second in‑person meeting with Iranian officials before the ceasefire expires next week. Preliminary discussions are under way on potential dates and locations, including Geneva and Islamabad. Turkey is working to bridge gaps between the two sides. Officials remain hopeful a diplomatic off‑ramp is achievable, and the ceasefire deadline could be extended to allow more time. @wfwitness
